_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of implementing Unix and Windows systems that provide radius authentication and logging, DNS, mail, web service, FTP server, DHCP, firewall, and SNMP.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, including file system structure, process management, user and permission models, package management, and common command-line utilities for text processing and system monitoring.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.505), TF-K-013 (TF, cosine: 0.482).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.0/10 (raw 0.301). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [authentication, dhcp, dns, firewall, ftp, implementing] vs WIDAI-only terms [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.866 (P=0.868, R=0.864). Balanced P/R — the 23-word DCWF element and 32-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.1%). "Knowledge of implementing Unix and Windows systems that provide radius authentic" and "Knowledge of Linux operating system fundamentals relevant to data engineering, i" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.510 > STS 0.301 (gap +0.209). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.5%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [authentication, dhcp, dns, firewall, ftp, implementing]. WIDAI-only: [command, common, data, engineering, file, fundamentals].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.209 with low STS 0.301. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
